mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises corporate discount rates and depresses earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns. The signal activates only when FEDFUNDS crosses above the 5.0% threshold.
failure_condition: If in any rolling 2-year window with FEDFUNDS > 5.0, the mean 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05) or if the signal produces positive returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market with UNRATE below 5%, a rising unemployment rate reduces wage inflation fears and diminishes the urgency of monetary tightening, driving positive forward equity returns. In a slack labor market (UNRATE above 6%), falling unemployment signals improving fundamentals and also boosts returns. The effect of UNRATE changes flips direction across these regimes.
failure_condition: If during tight-labor regimes (UNRATE < 5%) the correlation between monthly UNRATE changes and subsequent 60-day returns is not positive (or is negative and significant) over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. The signal is always active, based on the level of consumer spending.
failure_condition: If the 12-month rolling rank correlation between RSAFS level and subsequent 60-day returns falls below 0.03 for two consecutive years, or if the mean return during periods of rising RSAFS is not significantly greater than during falling periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is expanding (INDPRO rising) and the federal funds rate is low (FEDFUNDS below 4.5%), the economy enjoys accommodative monetary policy and real growth, producing positive equity returns. The combination produces a stronger signal than either series alone. The interaction is central, as favorable supply growth combined with cheap credit is especially bullish.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (INDPRO rising and FEDFUNDS low) does not produce a positive mean 20-day return that is significantly greater than the unconditional mean (p > 0.10), or if the interaction term in a linear regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, it signals a deterioration in labor market conditions that will reduce consumer income and spending, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns. The threshold crossing activates the signal.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 5.5% but the mean 5-day forward return over the following month is not negative (t-test p > 0.05), or if the hit rate (negative returns) is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a housing market downturn amplified by monetary tightening, which reduces construction activity, consumer wealth, and economic growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns. The joint worsening from both demand and monetary sides intensifies the negative signal.
failure_condition: If periods of simultaneous HOUST decline and FEDFUNDS increase do not produce a mean 60-day return significantly negative (p < 0.10), or if the interaction term in a regression is not significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing output and economic activity, which boosts corporate earnings and equity prices. The signal is always active, with higher INDPRO leading to positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If the 12-month rolling rank correlation between INDPRO level and subsequent 20-day returns falls below 0.05 for any two consecutive years, or if the mean return after INDPRO increases is not greater than after decreases (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 110, it signals an outright contraction in manufacturing activity that reduces corporate earnings and forward economic outlook, causing negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns. The threshold activation occurs when INDPRO crosses below this level.
failure_condition: If INDPRO drops below 110 but the mean 60-day forward return in the following quarter is not negative (t-test p > 0.05), or if more than 50% of such events produce positive returns.
